---
title: "My R Markdown Primer"
author: "Sydney Claudio"
date: "April 13, 2026"
output: html_document
---

# Introduction

R Markdown is a tool that allows users to combine narrative writing, R code, and output into a single document. This makes it easier to create reports that are both readable and reproducible. Instead of separating code and analysis, everything is kept in one place.

# Narrative Section

This is an example of a narrative paragraph written in Markdown. Markdown is simple to use and allows for formatting such as **bold text**, *italic text*, and headers. It is useful for writing reports because it keeps formatting clean and easy to read.

# Math Expressions

Here is an inline math expression: $\alpha + \beta = \gamma$

Here is a displayed equation:

$$
E = mc^2
$$

# Code Chunk 1: Load Data

```{r}
# Load built-in dataset
data(mtcars)

# View first few rows
head(mtcars)

# Summary of dataset
summary(mtcars)

# Basic plot
plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg,
     main = "Car Weight vs MPG",
     xlab = "Weight",
     ylab = "Miles Per Gallon")

# Conclusion Paragraph: Using R Markdown was a helpful experience because it allowed me to combine writing and code into one document. Compared to writing a plain report, R Markdown makes it easier to show data analysis alongside explanations. One challenge was learning the syntax for code chunks and formatting, but once understood, it became straightforward to use.
